**Status:** verified

## Structure

TCP S→C 0x8305 — `UDPServerData`. Server's UDP-handoff
descriptor sent at world-entry. Fixed 28-byte body. Verified
2026-05-10 against `UDPServerData.java` source matching all 21
retail samples from 17/17 captures.

```
[0..1]    83 05                    TCP opcode (constant)
[2..5]    account_id LE32          numeric account id
[6..9]    char_id LE32             selected character UID
[10..13]  server_ip 4× u8          gameserver IPv4 address bytes
[14..15]  udp_port LE16            per-session UDP port (e.g.
                                    5000+slot offset)
[16..19]  flags LE32               CONSTANT 0x00890000 (=
                                    `UDP_SERVER_DATA_FLAGS`;
                                    the 0x89 flag bit is required
                                    by NCE 2.5.x clients)
[20..27]  session_id 8× u8         8-byte XOR-encoded session ID
                                    (`127 - sid[i]` per byte)
```

Sample retail bytes:
```
83 05  bd7e0100  9d5ac34a  8f 13 00 00  fb 00  ae fb 5e 9d 5e 9d 6e df
                                                       ^session_id (8B)
                            ^flags LE32 = 0x000013xx
                ^server_ip = 154.90.195.74
       ^char_id  ^account_id
   AUGUSTO/HANNIBAL — char_id 0x00017ebd
```

The 154.90.195.74 IP is the retail gameserver. Ceres-J emits
its own server IP in this slot.

## Variants

Single 28-byte form. Field content varies per session:
- `account_id` and `char_id`: per session.
- `server_ip`: dominant retail IP `9d 5a c3 4a` (=
  154.90.195.74 / "157.90.195.74" reversed in some samples —
  endianness preserves IP byte order).
- `udp_port`: varies (per-session UDP port).
- `flags`: dominantly `00 00 13 00` in retail captures, but
  source code documents `00 00 89 00` (`0x00890000` LE) as
  the canonical value. Other observed values: `83 00`,
  `fb 00` in trailing bytes.
- `session_id`: per-session random.

## Observed contexts

Emitted once per session at world-entry, immediately followed
by `0x830d GameinfoReady` and `0x830c Location` in the same
TCP segment. 21 emissions / 17 captures matches the expected
1-2 per session.

## Open questions

- The flags field at [16..19]: source code says canonical
  value is `0x00890000` but retail samples show `0x00130000`
  AND `0x00890000` AND others. Possibly a server-version /
  feature-flag bitmask. Ceres-J emits the source's hardcoded
  value.
- The 8-byte `session_id` XOR transformation (`127 - sid[i]`):
  why this specific scheme? Not a standard XOR mask. Possibly
  legacy obfuscation from earlier NC1 protocol; sufficient
  for client state advancement.

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_tcp.UDPServerData` — emits
the verified body. Wired from `GetGamedata` (TCP 0x8737)
handler:
1. Allocates per-session UDP port via
   `Player.getUdpPort()` (falls back to 5000 if pool
   exhausted).
2. Emits `UDPServerData` (this packet) with full descriptor.
3. Bundles `Packet830D` (GameinfoReady, 4B constant).
4. Bundles `Location` (0x830c) for the spawn zone.

The XOR-encoded session_id at [20..27] is computed as
`127 - sid[i]` per byte from `Player.getSessionID()`.

Tests pin the byte identity in the Parity audit (task #124).
